fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Arkansas Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to Arkansas - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity arkansas, how to transfer pharmacist license to arkansas, pharmacist compact arkansas, arkansas pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: Arkansas Board of Pharmacy
board.mailing_address: 101 E. Capitol Ave., Suite 204, Little Rock, AR 72201

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in Arkansas.

steps.title: Create Account and Submit Online Application
steps.description: Register and complete the appropriate pharmacist application (e.g., by examination, by endorsement, by score transfer) in the ASBP Online Licensing Portal and pay the required application fee.

steps.title: Complete NABP Requirements and Exam Registration
steps.description: If applying by endorsement or score transfer, complete the NABP Record of Licensure Application. For all licensure types, register for the NAPLEX and/or the Arkansas Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) with NABP.

steps.title: Provide Official Documents
steps.description: Arrange for official verification of all original pharmacist licenses from other states (if applicable, typically through NABP) and ensure official college transcripts are sent directly to the Arkansas State Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Pass Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Take and pass the Arkansas Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE).

steps.title: Board Review and Approval
steps.description: The ASBP staff and Board will review your complete application and all supporting documentation once all requirements, including exam results, have been received.

steps.title: License Issuance
steps.description: Upon successful review and approval by the Board, your Arkansas Pharmacist license will be issued.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a Arkansas pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does Arkansas have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Arkansas. To work in Arkansas, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Arkansas?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Arkansas while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in Arkansas
hero.dek: To apply for a pharmacist license in Arkansas, applicants must register and complete their application through the ASBP Online Licensing Portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the ASBP Online Licensing Portal, click 'Register' for new users, create an account with your personal details, and then select the appropriate pharmacist application type to begin.
